curriculum_title: Mastering C Programming: Ultimate Systems, Network & Embedded Expert (V7.0 Production-Forged)
target_audience: Intermediate developers aiming for elite Systems, Network, Kernel, or Embedded Engineering roles.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of computer architecture (RAM, CPU)
Familiarity with the Linux command line (Bash/Zsh)

learning_outcomes: Set up a reproducible C development environment using Docker.
Master the 4-stage compilation pipeline.
Manipulate physical silicon registers using bitwise logic.
modules.mini_project: Build a command-line bitwise calculator (AND, OR, XOR, Shifts) that directly maps logic to simulated 8-bit registers.

days.title: Linux C Environment, Compilation Basics, and the GCC Driver
topics: Setting up a Linux Dev Container (Docker/VS Code)
The 4-stage compilation process (Preprocessor, Compiler, Assembler, Linker)
Using GCC/Clang via CLI (gcc -E, -S, -c, -o)
days.visual_representation: A flowchart showing the transformation from .c source code to a binary executable, highlighting intermediate files (.i, .s, .o).
days.assessment_check: Can compile a multi-file program from the CLI inside a Docker container.

days.title: Branching Architecture, CPU Pipelines, and Loop Unrolling
topics: Control flow to assembly (cmp, jmp)
Branch Predictor mechanics and pipeline stalls
Hardware loop optimization and Loop Unrolling
days.visual_representation: A diagram mapping a C 'switch' statement to an O(1) Assembly Jump Table.
days.assessment_check: Rewrite a sparse switch statement to eliminate Branch Predictor misses.

days.title: Integer Types, Signedness Overflow, and IEEE 754 Floating Point
topics: Two's Complement, Sign Extension, and Integer Promotion
Exact-width types (stdint.h) and size limits
IEEE 754 Floating Point approximations, NaN, and Epsilon checking
days.visual_representation: A memory map showing how negative signed integers overflow past INT_MAX.
days.assessment_check: Debug a silent failure caused by comparing a signed integer with an unsigned integer.

days.title: Bitwise Logic, Hardware Shift Operations, and Practical Bit Masking
topics: AND (&), OR (|), XOR (^), NOT (~)
Logical vs Arithmetic Bit shifting (<<, >>) and the Barrel Shifter
Read-Modify-Write (RMW) register masking patterns
days.visual_representation: A visual mapping of 8-bit registers showing bits changing state during masking and shifting operations.
resources: K&R The C Programming Language: Chapter 2.9
days.assessment_check: Write an in-place XOR swap and safely clear the 4th bit of a hardware register using an inverted mask.

learning_outcomes: Visualize Virtual Memory (Stack vs Data vs Heap).
Confidently use single and double pointers for memory mutation.
Manage contiguous Multi-Dimensional matrices and CPU Caching.
modules.mini_project: Develop a lightweight Matrix multiplier utilizing strict Row-Major pointer arithmetic for 100% Cache Line efficiency.

days.title: Virtual Memory Segments & Storage Classes
topics: Text, Data, BSS, Stack, and Heap topology
Linkage and lifetime (static, extern)
Thread-local storage (thread_local)
days.visual_representation: A vertical memory map diagram showing how the stack grows downward and the heap grows upward.
deprecated_features: The 'register' and 'auto' keywords (ignored by modern compilers)
days.assessment_check: Draw the exact memory segment destination for global extern, static local, and thread_local variables.

days.title: Pointer Mechanics, MMU Faults, and Strict Aliasing
topics: Address-of (&) and Dereference (*) physical hardware mechanics
Pointer scaling and arithmetic
Type Punning, Opaque Pointers (void*), and the Strict Aliasing Rule
days.visual_representation: A number line of memory addresses illustrating how pointer arithmetic scales physically by the type size.
days.assessment_check: Debug a -O3 optimization failure caused by a Strict Aliasing violation between an int* and float*.

days.title: Array Decay, Multi-Dimensional Matrix Caching, and VLAs
topics: Array decay across function boundaries
Row-Major Order, Contiguous memory, and CPU L1 Cache Line prefetching
The fatal Stack-Overflow risks of Variable Length Arrays (VLAs)
days.visual_representation: A diagram contrasting cache-friendly Row-Major traversal vs cache-hostile Column-Major traversal.
deprecated_features: VLAs (made optional in C11, banned in secure systems)
days.assessment_check: Refactor a column-major 2D array traversal to row-major to fix a 100x performance penalty.

days.title: Double Pointers, Arrays of Pointers (argv), and Out-Parameters
topics: Multiple indirection (**ptr) and pass-by-reference mutation
Jagged arrays and command-line argument routing (char *argv[])
Designing APIs with Out-Parameters to return multiple dynamic states
days.visual_representation: A chain of memory blocks demonstrating a pointer pointing to another pointer, which points to the data payload.
resources: Linux man pages: execve(2) to understand argv/envp memory setup
days.assessment_check: Write a function that safely allocates memory for a caller using a double pointer Out-Parameter.

learning_outcomes: Manipulate C-strings natively without buffer overflows.
Implement Test-Driven Development via GCC Linker seams.
Master X-Macros and advanced metaprogramming.
modules.mini_project: Build a robust string tokenizer and JSON key-value extractor verified entirely through Linker-mocked Unit Tests.

days.title: C-Strings, Boundary Safety, and Resilient Parsing
topics: String literals (.rodata) vs Stack Array mutation
Boundary-safe string handling (snprintf, strlcpy) vs legacy flaws (strcpy, strncat)
Thread-safe parsing and error checking (strtok_r, strtol)
days.visual_representation: Memory layout showing strcpy overflowing a buffer vs snprintf enforcing a safe truncation boundary.
days.assessment_check: Replace all unsafe atoi and strcpy calls in a packet parser with strtol and snprintf.

days.title: Assertions, GCC Linker Mocking, and Gcov
topics: Runtime vs Compile-time assertions (assert vs _Static_assert)
Mocking hardware dependencies via GCC Linker seams (-Wl,--wrap)
Generating and analyzing Code Coverage (Line vs Branch) with Gcov
days.visual_representation: A Linker redirection diagram showing a call to 'read_sensor' being intercepted and routed to '__wrap_read_sensor'.
days.assessment_check: Write a test harness that mocks a database connection using the --wrap flag to simulate an offline state.

days.title: Preprocessor Mechanics, Macros, and X-Macro Metaprogramming
topics: Include guards, ODR violations, and #pragma once
Macro Functions, do-while(0) idiom, Stringification (#), and Token Pasting (##)
The X-Macro pattern for automatic enum/string array synchronization
days.visual_representation: A flowchart of the X-Macro expansion, showing a single list generating both an Enum and an Array of Strings.
days.assessment_check: Implement an X-Macro list to generate an FSM state enum and a perfectly synced debug string array.

learning_outcomes: Control memory alignment explicitly to pack data architectures.
Serialize structures safely across disparate Endian hardware.
Automate the compilation pipeline using GNU Make.
modules.mini_project: Design a memory-dense telemetry struct, serialize it securely to Network Byte Order, and build it via an automated Makefile.

days.title: Struct Padding, Type Punning, and Endianness
topics: CPU word boundaries, padding bytes, and offsetof
Type Punning via Unions and strict aliasing exemptions
Network Byte Order (Big-Endian vs Little-Endian) and POSIX htonl/ntohl
days.visual_representation: A byte-grid diagram showing 'wasted' padding bytes inserted between a char and an int to satisfy hardware alignment.
deprecated_features: Unbounded struct bitfields for cross-platform network parsing
resources: The Lost Art of Structure Packing (Eric S. Raymond)
days.assessment_check: Reorder a 32-byte struct to completely eliminate 12 bytes of compiler-injected padding.

days.title: Function Pointers, FSMs, and the Compilation Pipeline
topics: Function pointer syntax and Indirect CPU Branch penalties
O(1) Finite State Machines using Arrays of Function Pointers (Jump Tables)
The 4-stage pipeline: Preprocessor, cc1 Compiler, Assembler, Linker
days.visual_representation: A sequence diagram mapping an Enum state directly to an array index, fetching the corresponding executable assembly address.
days.assessment_check: Replace a 20-case switch statement with an O(1) function pointer jump table.

days.title: Static vs Dynamic Libraries, GNU Make, and ELF Binaries
topics: Creating .a and .so files (-shared, -fPIC) and resolving LD_LIBRARY_PATH
Makefile Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs), Targets, and Automatic Variables
Dissecting the ELF Binary format (readelf, objdump, nm)
days.visual_representation: A memory topology contrasting static library binary bloat against a single Shared Object (.so) mapped in RAM.
days.assessment_check: Extract the Symbol Table from an ELF Object file using 'nm' to identify missing Linker dependencies.

learning_outcomes: Manage file/stream boundaries and OS buffering.
Implement Variadic Functions manually via stack scraping.
Bypass generic glibc allocations using custom Arena memory architectures.
modules.mini_project: Build an Opaque custom memory allocator (Arena) that processes variadic inputs safely without a single memory leak.

days.title: I/O Streams, Variadics, and Encapsulation
topics: Standard I/O buffering (stdout vs stderr) and fflush
Variadic Functions (stdarg.h, va_list) and ABI stack scraping
The pImpl Idiom: Perfect encapsulation via Opaque Pointers
days.visual_representation: A visual showing va_start pointing to the first argument, and va_arg jumping the pointer linearly down the hardware stack.
days.assessment_check: Write a custom variadic logger function that formats a string and immediately flushes it to a disk file.

days.title: Heap Internals: malloc, free, and Glibc Metadata
topics: The physics of dynamic memory (brk, sbrk, mmap)
Glibc malloc chunk metadata, sizing, and alignment
Heap fragmentation and allocation algorithms (First-Fit, Best-Fit)
days.visual_representation: A block diagram of a malloc Heap chunk, revealing the hidden 16-byte metadata header preceding the user's data pointer.
days.assessment_check: Calculate the exact physical RAM wasted by metadata overhead when allocating 10,000 separate 4-byte integers.

days.title: Memory Leaks, Dangling Pointers, and Use-After-Free
topics: Memory Leaks vs Resource Exhaustion
The mechanics of Dangling Pointers and Double Free aborts
Use-After-Free (UAF) exploitation theory
days.visual_representation: An execution timeline showing memory being freed, subsequently reallocated to an attacker's payload, and then dangerously manipulated via the original dangling pointer.
days.assessment_check: Identify and patch a subtle memory leak occurring exclusively inside an early-return error handling block.

days.title: Bypassing Malloc: Arena (Linear) Allocators and Object Pools
topics: Why glibc malloc is too slow for gaming and high-frequency trading
Architecting Arena (Linear) Allocators for O(1) allocation and 1-call teardowns
Object Pools / Slab Allocators for fragmentation-free fixed-size nodes
days.visual_representation: A memory map contrasting a fragmented standard heap with a contiguous Arena Allocator tailored for fast, localized object lifetimes.
days.assessment_check: Implement an Arena Allocator that pre-allocates 1MB and dishes out memory offsets sequentially.

learning_outcomes: Implement dynamic memory structures (Lists, Hashes).
Master the 'Intrusive' container architecture used by the Linux Kernel.
Handle catastrophic state failures utilizing setjmp/longjmp.
modules.mini_project: Build an Intrusive Hash Table leveraging Linux container_of macros for zero-allocation routing.

days.title: Building Cache-Friendly Linked Lists and Intrusive Structs
topics: Singly vs Doubly Linked Lists and Memory Ownership
The Cache-Miss problem of standard fragmented lists
Linux Kernel Intrusive Lists and the offsetof/container_of macro
days.visual_representation: A diagram comparing standard lists (nodes holding data pointers) to Intrusive lists (data holding node pointers).
days.assessment_check: Implement the container_of macro to extract a parent struct pointer from an embedded intrusive list node.

days.title: Hash Tables, Bitmaps, and Ring Buffers
topics: Hash Table string hashing (djb2) and collision chaining
Bitmaps for ultra-dense Boolean tracking
Ring Buffers (Circular Queues) for lock-free single-producer consumer tracking
days.visual_representation: A circular array diagram with Head and Tail pointers chasing each other indefinitely.
days.assessment_check: Write a Ring Buffer implementation that safely overwrites the oldest data when capacity is reached.

days.title: Extreme State Exits: Exceptions in C via setjmp and longjmp
topics: Simulating try/catch Exception Handling in C
The setjmp/longjmp context saving mechanics
Stack Unwinding failures and volatile register preservation
days.visual_representation: A call-stack diagram demonstrating longjmp bypassing normal function returns, jumping instantly back down the stack frame history.
days.assessment_check: Utilize setjmp/longjmp to immediately abort a deep recursive parsing function upon encountering a fatal syntax error.

learning_outcomes: Interface directly with the Linux Kernel via Syscalls.
Achieve Zero-Copy data transfers using Memory Mapped files (mmap).
Clone processes, execute new binaries, and handle asynchronous hardware signals.
modules.mini_project: Develop a background Daemon process that memory-maps a hardware file, detaches from the terminal, and safely traps SIGINT to exit.

days.title: The Virtual File System (VFS), File Descriptors, and ioctl
topics: The 'Everything is a File' UNIX philosophy
File Descriptors vs C Streams (FILE*)
Configuring hardware devices and terminal TTYs using ioctl
days.visual_representation: An architecture diagram mapping User-Space File Descriptors through the Kernel VFS down to physical hardware drivers.
resources: Advanced Programming in the UNIX Environment (APUE)
days.assessment_check: Extract the raw File Descriptor integer from a FILE* and use ioctl to query the exact pixel dimensions of the terminal.

days.title: POSIX Raw I/O (read, write, open) vs C Standard I/O
topics: Invoking Syscalls: open, read, write, close
Unbuffered I/O mechanics and Context Switch overhead
File permissions and the umask
days.visual_representation: A timeline contrasting the latency of 1,000 unbuffered 1-byte write() syscalls vs 1 buffered 1,000-byte fwrite() call.
days.assessment_check: Rewrite a file parsing utility to use raw POSIX open() and read() syscalls directly, handling partial reads safely.

days.title: Memory Mapping Files and Devices via mmap()
topics: Zero-Copy Architecture: Bypassing the Kernel I/O buffer
Mapping files directly into Virtual RAM using mmap
Page alignment, PROT_READ/WRITE, and MAP_SHARED vs MAP_PRIVATE
days.visual_representation: A memory map showing mmap bypassing the standard read/write OS buffer, pointing the user's Virtual RAM directly to the Disk Cache.
days.assessment_check: Map a 1GB file into RAM using mmap and modify a specific byte offset without issuing a single write() syscall.

days.title: Process Cloning: The fork() and execve() Architecture
topics: The mechanics of fork(): Copy-on-Write (CoW) memory spaces
Branching parent vs child execution paths
Overwriting the process image using the execve() family
days.visual_representation: A process tree diagram showing a Parent invoking fork() to clone itself, followed by the Child invoking execve() to load a new binary.
days.assessment_check: Write a program that uses fork() and execve() to spawn a child process that executes the 'ls -la' system command.

days.title: Process State: Zombies, Orphans, and waitpid()
topics: The lifecycle of a Process and the PID table
Reaping exit statuses using wait() and waitpid()
Zombie processes (unreaped children) vs Orphan processes (dead parents)
days.visual_representation: A state machine diagram showing process execution states: Running, Sleeping, Stopped, and Zombie.
days.assessment_check: Write a parent process that spawns 5 children and uses waitpid() to systematically collect and log all 5 exit codes.

days.title: Asynchronous Interrupts: Signal Handling (SIGINT, SIGSEGV)
topics: Hardware interrupts routed to Software Signals
Trapping signals using sigaction (vs deprecated signal())
Reentrancy rules: What functions are Async-Signal-Safe?
days.visual_representation: An execution timeline showing the main thread abruptly halted by the Kernel to execute the Signal Handler function asynchronously.
deprecated_features: The legacy signal() function (Unreliable across OS flavors)
days.assessment_check: Implement a signal handler utilizing sigaction that safely catches SIGINT (Ctrl+C) to close database connections before exiting.

days.title: Daemonization: Detaching from TTYs and Background Services
topics: The Double-Fork architecture
Creating a new Session ID (setsid) to detach from the terminal
Closing standard file descriptors and redirecting to /dev/null
days.visual_representation: A flowchart detailing the precise POSIX steps required to perfectly orphan a process into a background Daemon.
deprecated_features: daemon() (often considered non-standard or problematic in systemd environments)
days.assessment_check: Write a C program that daemonizes itself, detaches from the CLI, and writes a heartbeat log to /var/log/syslog every 10 seconds.

learning_outcomes: Architect highly concurrent, thread-safe software.
Debug Race Conditions, Deadlocks, and Priority Inversions.
Pass binary payloads across process boundaries at massive speeds.
modules.mini_project: Build an ultra-fast IPC Producer/Consumer pipeline sharing an mmap'd Ring Buffer across two isolated processes via POSIX Semaphores.

days.title: Thread Spawning (pthreads), Context Switching, and Shared Memory Constraints
topics: Processes vs Threads: Shared Heap/Data vs Private Stacks
Spawning and joining threads using pthread_create
Context Switching overhead in the CPU scheduler
days.visual_representation: A memory map contrasting two isolated Processes vs two Threads sharing the exact same Global memory and Heap.
days.assessment_check: Spawn 4 worker threads that each process a quadrant of a massive array concurrently, joining their results in the main thread.

days.title: Mutexes, Semaphores, Condition Variables, and Deadlocks
topics: Mutual Exclusion (pthread_mutex_t) to prevent Data Races
Signaling state changes using Condition Variables (pthread_cond_t)
Resource tracking with Semaphores and debugging Deadlocks / Priority Inversion
days.visual_representation: A state diagram showing a thread sleeping on a Condition Variable lock until explicitly signaled by a worker thread.
days.assessment_check: Implement a Thread-Safe Job Queue using a Mutex to protect the data and a Condition Variable to wake up sleeping workers.

days.title: Cross-Process Routing: Anonymous Pipes, FIFOs, and Message Queues
topics: Unidirectional byte streams using pipe()
Named pipes (FIFOs) persisting in the filesystem
POSIX Message Queues for structured, prioritized datagram passing
days.visual_representation: An architecture diagram showing a Parent and Child process writing and reading from opposing ends of a unidirectional Pipe.
days.assessment_check: Create an Anonymous Pipe before forking, then have the child process stream file data through the pipe to the parent.

days.title: Ultra-Fast IPC: POSIX Shared Memory and Unix Domain Sockets
topics: Setting up shared RAM blocks using shm_open and mmap
Synchronizing cross-process memory using process-shared Semaphores
Unix Domain Sockets (AF_UNIX) for bidirectional, zero-network local routing
days.visual_representation: A RAM diagram showing two distinct Virtual Memory spaces pointing their Page Tables to the exact same physical RAM Shared Memory block.
days.assessment_check: Write two separate C binaries. Process A maps a Shared Memory block and writes a struct. Process B maps it and reads the struct instantly.

learning_outcomes: Understand the OSI model and socket APIs.
Implement robust, persistent TCP Streams and fast UDP Datagrams.
Overcome network blocking with fcntl.
modules.mini_project: Write a multi-client TCP Chat Server capable of serializing custom binary message structs and broadcasting them across sockets.

days.title: The OSI Model, IP/Port Binding, and building a TCP Server
topics: The Berkeley Sockets API (socket, bind, listen, accept)
TCP state mechanics (SYN, ACK) and reliable stream guarantees
Handling endianness with sockaddr_in structures
days.visual_representation: A chronological sequence diagram illustrating the Client connect() handshake synchronizing with the Server accept() API.
days.assessment_check: Build a basic TCP Echo Server that binds to port 8080, accepts a client connection, and echoes strings back.

days.title: UDP Datagrams, Packet Fragmentation, and Binary Serialization
topics: Connectionless transmission using UDP (sendto, recvfrom)
MTU limits, packet fragmentation, and out-of-order delivery
Serializing C structs securely to raw byte buffers for network transport
days.visual_representation: A comparison diagram showing TCP's ordered byte-stream vs UDP's chaotic, isolated datagram delivery.
days.assessment_check: Serialize a Game State struct into a byte array, transmit it via UDP, and accurately deserialize it on the receiver.

days.title: Blocking vs Non-Blocking I/O (fcntl and EAGAIN)
topics: The CPU stall of blocking recv() and accept() calls
Using fcntl to switch sockets to O_NONBLOCK mode
Trapping EAGAIN / EWOULDBLOCK error codes to resume processing
days.visual_representation: A timeline contrasting a blocked thread waiting for network data vs a non-blocking thread checking the socket and moving on instantly.
days.assessment_check: Refactor a TCP socket to Non-Blocking mode and handle the EAGAIN error gracefully without crashing.

learning_outcomes: Detect and eliminate memory corruption using runtime sanitizers.
Automate vulnerability discovery using Fuzzing tools.
Enforce strict MISRA C guidelines for safety-critical logic.
modules.mini_project: Set up a complete AFL++ fuzzing harness for a custom text parser, discover a hidden buffer overflow, and patch the C code using ASan logs.

days.title: Dynamic Analysis: Valgrind, AddressSanitizer (ASan), and ThreadSanitizer
topics: Memory profiling and leak detection via Valgrind's Memcheck
Compiling with GCC Sanitizers (-fsanitize=address, undefined, thread)
Interpreting ASan Red-Zone shadow memory crash reports
days.visual_representation: A shadow memory diagram illustrating how ASan poisons the bytes immediately surrounding an array to catch out-of-bounds reads.
days.assessment_check: Compile a buggy C program using AddressSanitizer and successfully identify the exact line number of a Use-After-Free violation.

days.title: Automated Vulnerability Discovery: Fuzzing via AFL++
topics: The concept of Coverage-Guided Genetic Fuzzing
Compiling with afl-gcc to inject branch-tracking instrumentation
Writing efficient Fuzz Harnesses that consume raw byte mutation streams
days.visual_representation: A workflow diagram showing AFL++ mutating inputs, feeding them into the target binary, mapping the execution branches, and saving inputs that trigger new paths.
days.assessment_check: Execute an AFL++ fuzzer against a vulnerable binary parser and isolate the specific mutated input file that triggers the SIGSEGV.

days.title: Safety-Critical C: The MISRA C Standard
topics: Core principles of MISRA C:2012 for automotive and aerospace systems
Banning dynamic memory (malloc) and variable length arrays (VLAs)
Integrating static analysis tools (cppcheck, clang-tidy) to enforce compliance
days.visual_representation: A terminal output snippet of clang-tidy aggressively highlighting MISRA rule violations regarding implicit type conversions.
days.assessment_check: Run cppcheck with MISRA flags enabled on a previous project and rewrite the code to resolve all static analysis warnings.

learning_outcomes: Control CPU hardware directly using Inline Assembly.
Understand the mechanics of booting a system without an OS.
Architect Bare-Metal Linker Scripts for Microcontrollers.
modules.mini_project: Write a functional ARM Cortex-M bootloader sequence using a custom `.ld` script and Inline Assembly to set up the Stack Pointer and zero the BSS.

days.title: Inline Assembly: Interacting with CPU Registers natively in GCC
topics: GCC Extended Asm syntax (asm volatile)
Mapping C variables directly to hardware input/output registers
Clobber lists and protecting the CPU pipeline from optimizer assumptions
days.visual_representation: A diagram demonstrating the syntax mapping of C variables into the %0 and %1 operands of a raw Assembly instruction block.
resources: GCC Manual: How to Use Inline Assembly Language in C Code
days.assessment_check: Write an Inline Assembly block that executes the x86 CPUID instruction to extract the processor's vendor string into a C char array.

days.title: OS Bypassing: Writing Bare-Metal Linker Scripts (.ld)
topics: The boot sequence without an OS (Reset Vectors and CRT0)
Writing a Linker Script to physically map .text to Flash ROM and .data to SRAM
Defining linker symbols (e.g., _sbss, _ebss) for C code to reference during boot initialization
days.visual_representation: A physical memory map of a Microcontroller, showing the Linker Script directing executable code to Flash and writable variables to RAM.
days.assessment_check: Write a minimal .ld Linker Script that explicitly defines the Entry Point and forces the .text section to start at address 0x08000000.

learning_outcomes: Utilize AVX/SSE Compiler Intrinsics for parallel math.
Implement non-blocking concurrency via C11 Atomics.
Architect an asynchronous Event Loop capable of resolving the C10K problem.
modules.mini_project: The Capstone: Architect a Single-Threaded epoll Web Server utilizing Arena Memory Allocators, capable of handling 10,000 concurrent sockets without Mutex locks.

days.title: SIMD Vectorization (AVX/SSE) via Compiler Intrinsics
topics: The concept of Single Instruction, Multiple Data (SIMD)
Utilizing AVX2/AVX-512 Intrinsics (_mm256_add_epi32) to process arrays concurrently
Data alignment requirements (_mm_malloc) for hardware vector registers
days.visual_representation: A processor diagram contrasting standard ALU sequential execution vs a Vector Register processing 8 integers in a single clock cycle.
days.assessment_check: Rewrite a standard 'for' loop that adds two large arrays together to use AVX2 Intrinsics, achieving a 4x+ speedup.

days.title: Lock-Free Programming: C11 Atomics (stdatomic.h) and Memory Order
topics: The _Atomic keyword and hardware Compare-And-Swap (CAS) loops
Bypassing Mutex bottlenecks in highly concurrent queues
Memory Ordering semantics (Acquire, Release, Seq_Cst) and CPU instruction reordering
days.visual_representation: A timeline showing multiple threads executing a CAS loop natively in hardware, resolving a race condition instantly without OS-level Mutex sleep states.
days.assessment_check: Implement a perfectly thread-safe, lock-free global counter that multiple pthreads increment simultaneously using atomic fetches.

days.title: Hardware Concurrency Bugs: Cache Coherency, False Sharing, and the ABA Problem
topics: The MESI Cache Coherence Protocol across multi-core architectures
Diagnosing False Sharing: Threads thrashing the same 64-byte Cache Line
The ABA Problem in Lock-Free structures and mitigation theory
days.visual_representation: A CPU diagram illustrating two cores violently invalidating each other's L1 caches because they are mutating independent variables that share the same physical cache line.
resources: Intel 64 Architecture Software Developer's Manual (Cache Mechanics)
days.assessment_check: Use the alignas() keyword to force two independently threaded variables onto separate 64-byte cache boundaries, eliminating False Sharing.

days.title: Introduction to epoll: The Event-Driven Linux Architecture
topics: The C10K Problem: Why 'One Thread per Client' architecture fails at scale
The epoll API (epoll_create, epoll_ctl, epoll_wait)
Edge-Triggered (EPOLLET) vs Level-Triggered notification states
days.visual_representation: An architecture diagram showing a single Event Loop (epoll_wait) efficiently managing thousands of non-blocking sockets.
days.assessment_check: Configure an epoll instance to monitor 3 non-blocking sockets and print a message exactly when a socket becomes readable.

days.title: Capstone: Architecting a Single-Threaded epoll Web Server
topics: Combining epoll, non-blocking fcntl sockets, and robust parsing into a monolithic engine
Implementing an Arena Allocator to manage per-request HTTP parsing without heap fragmentation
Executing load testing (Apache Bench / wrk) to verify lock-free, zero-leak performance at 10,000+ RPS
days.visual_representation: A comprehensive system blueprint mapping incoming network packets through the epoll event loop, into the Arena memory space, and back out via the network socket.
days.assessment_check: Successfully build, compile, and benchmark the C Web Server, achieving 0 memory leaks in ASan under extreme load tests.
